result:
Implemented `serve --watch` so the web UI can follow on-disk note edits without restarting. The server now has shared notebook state and a polling watcher that fingerprints `notes/*.md`, rebuilds the snapshot when files change, and emits SSE refresh events from `/events`; rendered pages opt into an `EventSource` reload hook when watch mode is enabled. The core changes are in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L80), [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L790), [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L962), [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L2628), and [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L3848). I also kept non-watch mode rebuilding per request so existing behavior doesn’t regress.

I added coverage for the new flag, snapshot refresh, and SSE notifications in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go#L1886), and documented the new mode in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md#L36) and [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md#L64).

I could not run `go test` or `gofmt` here because the Go toolchain is not installed in this environment.
